Now VP JD Vance confirming that a tentative deal was reached between the US and Iran feels like a major turning point for traders watching $WTI.

From what’s being discussed so far: • The Strait of Hormuz could reopen
• Partial sanctions may get eased
• A 60-day truce for nuclear talks could begin

But according to Vance, a few wording issues still remain, and the final approval reportedly depends on Trump.

What makes this interesting is how quickly sentiment changed: • Earlier this week, oil dumped after optimism around negotiations. 

• Then military escalation pushed oil back higher again 
• Now traders are trying to price in whether this deal actually survives

Personally, my oil outlook is becoming slightly more bearish IF the agreement gets finalized because supply fears would likely cool down quickly.

That’s why I’m still watching $WTI closely on Bitget this weekend.

$BTC continues ranging.

Bitcoin is ranging since February 7th, and you can see how clean it's respected.

As you can see, I highlighted the range high and low, and three deviations outside the range.

What are range deviations? These are price moves where bigger players fill their orders.

For example, the bullish deviations below the rangelow. They trap shorts and breakout traders by consolidating below the rangelow. They use this liquidity to fill their orders and then price goes back up.

The same goes for the bearish deviation, people get trapped thinking it's a breakout. Bigger players absorb this liquidity to fill shorts, and boom, we drop back into the range.

Shorts printed well the last couple of days, and I closed most of them for 60%+.

My trading plan for next week is to add shorts if we get a higher retest, and I'll target the rangelow.

When we revisit the rangelow, I'll look for high-probability reversals to enter longs.

Have a great weekend everyone.
